parts.title: The Workshop
parts.text: Ruben, Khadija, and Hiro spent every Saturday in their inventor's workshop, a cluttered garage filled with tools and wild ideas. Their latest project was a **prototype** of a machine that could water gardens using an **irrigation** system powered by sunlight. Khadija built a strange-looking **contraption** from old bicycle parts and copper pipes.

parts.title: The Science Behind It
parts.text: Hiro explained that heat from the sun would cause water to **condense** inside a glass tube, creating droplets that dripped into the garden. Ruben studied the full **spectrum** of light with a prism to find which colors produced the most heat. He needed to **calibrate** the sensors so they turned on at exactly the right temperature. The idea was still **theoretical**, meaning it existed only on paper.

parts.title: The First Test
parts.text: When they tested the machine, a spark caused a brief flash of **combustion** inside the fuel chamber. Khadija added a chemical **catalyst** to speed up the reaction and keep the engine running. Water shot through the pipes at surprising **velocity**, much faster than expected. They noticed brown **corrosion** forming on the metal joints where water had leaked. Hiro called it a remarkable **phenomenon** that none of them had witnessed before.

parts.title: Back to the Drawing Board
parts.text: After the test, the three inventors reviewed their notes. Some parts worked perfectly, while others needed improvement. Ruben suggested replacing the rusted metal with plastic, and Khadija sketched a better pump design. They agreed that inventing was not about getting it right the first time — it was about learning and trying again.

definitions.meaning1: A fact, event, or situation that can be observed and studied. Example: A rainbow is a natural phenomenon caused by sunlight passing through raindrops.
definitions.meaning2: A remarkable or extraordinary person or thing. Example: The young singer was considered a phenomenon after her first concert.
definitions.studentChallenge: The northern lights are a beautiful natural ___ that lights up the sky.

definitions.meaning1: The process of burning, in which a substance reacts with oxygen and produces heat and light. Example: The combustion of wood in the fireplace kept the cabin warm all winter.
definitions.studentChallenge: The engine works by using ___ to burn fuel and create energy.

definitions.meaning1: A substance that speeds up a chemical reaction without being used up itself. Example: The scientist added a catalyst to make the experiment finish faster.
definitions.meaning2: Something that causes an important change or event to happen. Example: The new park became a catalyst for bringing the neighborhood together.
definitions.studentChallenge: Adding the special powder acted as a ___ that made the reaction happen in seconds.

definitions.meaning1: The speed of something moving in a particular direction. Example: The velocity of the roller coaster increased as it raced down the steep hill.
definitions.studentChallenge: The rocket reached an incredible ___ as it blasted off into space.

definitions.meaning1: To adjust an instrument or tool so it gives accurate measurements. Example: The scientist had to calibrate the thermometer before recording any temperatures.
definitions.studentChallenge: You need to ___ the scale before weighing the ingredients for the recipe.

definitions.meaning1: To change from a gas or vapor into a liquid, usually by cooling. Example: Water droplets condense on the outside of a cold glass on a hot day.
definitions.meaning2: To make something shorter or smaller. Example: The teacher asked us to condense our reports to one page.
definitions.studentChallenge: On chilly mornings, you can see steam ___ into tiny water droplets on the window.

definitions.meaning1: The band of colors produced when white light is separated, such as in a rainbow. Example: A prism splits light into every color of the spectrum.
definitions.meaning2: A complete range of something. Example: The students shared a wide spectrum of opinions during the class debate.
definitions.studentChallenge: When sunlight passes through a prism, it breaks into the full ___ of colors.

definitions.meaning1: Based on ideas or possibilities rather than actual experience or proof. Example: The plan was only theoretical because no one had tried it in real life yet.
definitions.studentChallenge: The idea sounds great, but it is still ___ and has never been tested.

definitions.meaning1: The gradual destruction of metal or other material by a chemical reaction, such as rust. Example: Years of corrosion had turned the iron gate a reddish-brown color.
definitions.studentChallenge: The old bridge showed signs of ___ where the metal had rusted and weakened.

definitions.meaning1: A machine or device that looks strange or complicated. Example: The inventor built a wild contraption out of gears, springs, and rubber bands.
definitions.studentChallenge: He built a funny-looking ___ out of cardboard tubes and tape to launch paper airplanes.

definitions.meaning1: The system or process of supplying water to land or crops through pipes, channels, or sprinklers. Example: Farmers use irrigation to keep their fields watered during the dry season.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ system delivered water to every row of crops in the field.

definitions.meaning1: The first model of something, built to test an idea before making the final version. Example: The engineer built a prototype of the new car before the factory started production.
definitions.studentChallenge: Before manufacturing the toy, the company tested a ___ to make sure it worked properly.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A rainbow is a natural _______ caused by sunlight passing through tiny water droplets in the air.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The young gymnast was considered a _______ after winning three gold medals at just twelve years old.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ of gasoline inside the engine is what makes a car move forward.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The campers watched the _______ of dry leaves as the fire crackled and grew in the fire pit.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The scientist added a _______ to the mixture to speed up the chemical reaction.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The new community garden became a _______ for bringing neighbors together for the first time.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ of the baseball increased as it flew off the bat and sailed over the fence.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Scientists measured the _______ of the wind during the hurricane to determine its strength.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The mechanic needed to _______ the speedometer so it would show the correct speed.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Before the experiment began, the students had to _______ the measuring tools for accurate results.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: On cold mornings, water vapor will _______ on the inside of car windows, making them foggy.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The editor asked the writer to _______ the long article into a shorter summary for the newspaper.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A prism splits white light into the full _______ of colors from red to violet.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The art show featured a wide _______ of styles, from realistic paintings to abstract sculptures.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The plan to build a city on Mars is still _______ because no one has actually tried it.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Her ideas about time travel were interesting but entirely _______, with no real proof behind them.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Years of _______ had eaten away at the ship's metal hull, leaving it weak and full of holes.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The plumber warned that _______ inside the old pipes could cause a leak if they were not replaced.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The inventor built a funny-looking _______ out of tin cans, wire, and rubber bands.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Nobody believed the odd _______ would actually work until it started pouring water perfectly.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The farmers depended on an _______ system to water their crops during the long, dry summer.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Ancient civilizations built clever _______ channels to bring water from rivers to their farmland.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The engineer tested the _______ of the new airplane wing before building the full-size version.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The students presented a working _______ of their solar-powered fan at the science fair.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Hiro had always dreamed of building machines, so when his school announced an invention fair, he knew exactly what to create. He wanted to build a _(1)_ of a device that could help farmers water their crops more easily. The idea was to create a solar-powered _(2)_ system that would deliver water straight to the roots of plants.

First, Hiro assembled a strange-looking _(3)_ from old pipes, a small motor, and a glass tube. He used a prism to study the _(4)_ of light and figure out which colors carried the most energy. Then he had to _(5)_ the temperature sensor so it would activate at exactly the right moment. His plan was still _(6)_ — it looked great on paper, but he had not tested it yet.

During his first test, a tiny spark caused a flash of _(7)_ inside the motor chamber. Hiro quickly added a chemical _(8)_ to keep the reaction going at a steady pace. Water shot through the tubes at high _(9)_, spraying across the table. He also spotted brown _(10)_ forming on the metal fittings where moisture had collected. Steam began to _(11)_ on the cool glass, forming tiny droplets that rolled down the side.

The judges called Hiro's invention a true _(12)_ — something remarkable that none of them had ever seen at a school fair before. He won first place and a chance to present his design at the city science museum.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
